item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose the statement from the start and keep a rights-first position throughout. Agent A consistently says society matters but should not override individual rights as a general rule. Agent B starts opposed and becomes slightly more emphatic by turns 4 and 6, but this is intensification rather than a flip. Both agents engage the common-good, minority-rights, accountability, and abuse-of-power points, but the later turns largely reinforce shared ground rather than rebut sharply.
